content: don't you have the balance via `builder.balance`?

author: potuz
content: I probably don't understand the format of the API then. It said the index and status. I guess it means that it returns the builder object plus those two then?

author: potuz
content: Ah right

author: potuz
content: Then it's fine can I implement that already?

author: nflaig
content: would be great if we have a poc implementation for it

author: nflaig
content: need review on `BuilderStatus`, the current approach allows us to ssz encode it vs. the approach you had Potuz with the enum string does not

author: nflaig
content: also what builder statuses actually exist, but `pending`, `active`, `withdrawn` seems right, there can't be `exited` due to index reuse I suppose

author: potuz
content: withdrawn probably also can' t exist

author: potuz
content: because you will be forced to keep the pubkey forever

author: potuz
content: and we will definitely remove it

author: nflaig
content: so if no new builders join, a previous fully withdrawn builder would still be part of the state right?

author: nflaig
content: a `withdrawn` builder would be one that has `builder.withdrawable_epoch != FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH` I suppose, as this one is neither pending nor active

author: potuz
content: that' s not withdrawn but rather exited

author: potuz
content: those I think it' s fine to signal

author: potuz
content: but as soon as it' s withdrawn (= builder.withdrawable_epoch < currentEpoch) then those builders will most likely be gone from the state so better to not return that

author: nflaig
content: so `pending`, `active`, `exited`?

author: potuz
content: and I guess an error for not found or whatever

author: nflaig
content: so this case is a bit random, if `builder.withdrawable_epoch <= get_current_epoch(state)` then it can be still part of the state but `exited` or a completely new builder can be on the exact same index

author: potuz
content: yes

author: nflaig
content:
the PR says the following which mirror the validators api we have
```
If an index or public key does not match any known builder, no information will be returned but this will not cause an error.
```
this seems fine to me, you don't want that api to throw an error if one builder is missing, or at least for validators, we didn't want that
